commit.message:
fix(session): guard async busy lock with pcall to prevent permanent deadlock (#21)

All three async.void functions (select_tool, select_session, rename_session)
called the inner _() function bare. If ui_select or ui_input (both async.wrap
callbacks) threw a runtime error, state.busy would remain true permanently,
deadlocking the plugin until Neovim restart.

- Replace bare _() with pcall(_) in select_tool, select_session, rename_session
- Ensure state.busy = false always executes on both success and error paths
- Notify the user with [aiwaku] prefixed ERROR message when an unexpected error occurs
- Add missing ---@async annotation to select_session and rename_session

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-refresh JWT token in background when user roles or role permissions change (#356)

* Initial plan

* feat: auto-refresh JWT token when user roles or role permissions change

- UpdateUserRoles now regenerates security stamp and invalidates cache after
  successful role update, causing the user's next request to get a 401 which
  the frontend interceptor handles by calling RefreshToken
- EditRole now finds all users assigned the edited role and invalidates their
  security stamp caches so their tokens are refreshed with updated permissions
- SecurityStampValidationTransform in gateway now skips stamp validation for
  RefreshToken endpoint to avoid infinite 401 loops during token refresh
- UsersService.RefreshToken now correctly fetches the user with roles/permissions
  and generates a fresh token (removes broken RefreshTokenAsync call that
  compared access token against stored refresh token, always failing)
- Adds tests: UpdateUserRoles_RegeneratesSecurityStampAndInvalidatesCache and
  EditRole_RegeneratesSecurityStampForAffectedUsers

- RolesService.AddRole caches DTOs instead of entities in the All-roles cache
- RolesService.DeleteRole uses RoleWithUsersSpec with null-safe Users check

* fix: correctly extract LoginResponseDTO from API response wrapper in refresh token interceptor

The v1UsersRefreshTokenList() endpoint returns LoginResponseDTOResponse
(shape: { success, data: LoginResponseDTO, message, errors }).
The interceptor was casting response.data as LoginResponseDTO, so user/
accessToken/refreshToken all resolved to undefined → null, clearing
localStorage on every successful token refresh.

Fix: access response.data.data (the inner LoginResponseDTO) so the correct
fields are read and localStorage is updated instead of cleared.

fix(session): reopen sidebar in current tab on toggle (#19)

Previously, state.win_id stored a single global window handle with no
awareness of which tabpage it belonged to. win_visible() only checked
nvim_win_is_valid(), so a sidebar open in tab 1 was still treated as
"visible" when the user moved to tab 2. The first toggle from tab 2
therefore closed the tab 1 window and returned early, requiring a
second toggle to actually open the sidebar in tab 2.

Changes:
- window.lua: add win_visible_in_current_tab() that checks both window
  validity and current tabpage membership.
- session.lua: add local close_sidebar_window() helper; switch
  toggle() and open_session() to use win_visible_in_current_tab() so
  the sidebar is treated as hidden when it lives in another tab.
  open_session() closes any existing sidebar window before opening a
  fresh split in the current tab, moving the sidebar in one press.
  new_session() and clear_context() use the new helper for consistent
  unconditional close behaviour.
- send.lua: switch send_to_session() to win_visible_in_current_tab()
  so send_selection() and send_buffer() also auto-open in the current
  tab rather than acting on a sidebar that exists only in another tab.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786) (#1492)

* fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786)

When go-to-definition is used on a type that lives in a different
assembly from the one FCS reports (e.g. System.Boolean forwarded from
netstandard.dll to System.Runtime.dll), ILSpy's CSharpDecompiler
throws:
  'Decompiling types that are not part of the main module is not supported'

This happens because ILSpy's type system resolves type forwarders
transparently — GetTypeDefinition returns the actual type definition
from the target assembly's module, but the decompiler was created for
the source assembly. The mismatch causes the exception.

Fix: after resolving the type definition, check whether its
ParentModule matches the decompiler's main module. If not (a type
forwarder), retrieve the actual assembly path from
IModule.PEFile.FileName and create a fresh CSharpDecompiler for
that assembly. Resolve the type definition again in the new type
system, then decompile as normal.

If the PEFile path is unavailable or the type cannot be resolved in

[Repo Assist] fix: skip unreadable files in rename; remove duplicate log in textDocument/implementation (#1491)

* fix: skip unreadable files in rename and log warnings in textDocument/implementation

Fixes two code quality issues in AdaptiveFSharpLspServer.fs:

1. TextDocumentRename: skip files that cannot be read
   When building workspace edits for rename, if a file cannot be opened
   (e.g., SourceLinked virtual paths like /_/src/Server.fs from external
   packages that FCS references but don't exist locally), return None and
   filter it out rather than generating edits for an unreadable file.
   This prevents client errors when the LSP client tries to apply edits
   to files that don't exist. See issue #1011.

2. TextDocumentImplementation: remove duplicate log + log warning on failure
   - Remove the duplicate 'TextDocumentImplementation Request: {params}'
     log that was emitted twice in the handler.
   - Log a warning when getProjectOptions fails instead of silently calling
     failwith, making these failures visible in server logs.

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation

commit.message:
Implement Entity Source Generator

Implement the 5 core files of the entity source generator:

- EntityGenerator.cs: Main entry using ForAttributeWithMetadataName to
  discover [Entity] classes, parse columns/indexes/DetailOf attributes,
  map CLR types to PostgreSQL types, and invoke all 4 emitters

- DalEmitter.cs: Generates CREATE TABLE DDL with proper column types,
  NOT NULL constraints, PRIMARY KEY, PARTITION BY LIST for tenant tables,
  audit table DDL, and CREATE INDEX statements

- CrudEmitter.cs: Generates INSERT/SELECT/DELETE SQL constants,
  BuildInsertParams helper with proper NpgsqlParameter/NpgsqlDbType
  mapping, and ReadEntity method with correct reader methods

- AuditCrudEmitter.cs: Generates audit table name constant and
  DiffAuditSnapshots method using System.Text.Json.JsonDocument for
  NativeAOT-safe snapshot comparison

- DescEmitter.cs: Generates table name/tenant constants, Fields nested
  class with column name constants, DictFieldMetas dictionary with
  YTFieldMeta entries, and GetFieldMeta lookup method

Also adds <Compile Remove="Generated/**" /> to the sample project to
prevent double-compilation of emitted files.

fix(frontend): fix blank screen on summary page after FastAPI migration

Three bugs caused a white crash screen on /summary/:id:

1. SummaryView: isLoading initialised to false, so Summary rendered
   immediately with an empty {} object, calling formatDate(undefined)
   which throws RangeError — fixed by initialising isLoading to true
2. SummaryView: currentSummary.length === 0 always false on an object
   (undefined !== 0) — fixed with !currentSummary.id guard
3. Field name mismatch: Flask returned `text`, FastAPI returns `content`
   — updated SummaryView, Summary, and TypingSummary to use `content`
   (Summary retains `?? summary.text` fallback for safety)
4. formatDate: added null guard to prevent RangeError on invalid dates

commit.message:
fix(config): resolve CORS_ORIGINS SettingsError on startup

pydantic-settings v2 calls json.loads() on list[str] fields before
field_validator runs, causing a SettingsError if the env var is not
valid JSON (e.g. plain URL or comma-separated string in a dashboard).

- Change CORS_ORIGINS type from list[str] to str to bypass pydantic-
  settings' complex-type JSON pre-parsing
- Replace field_validator with a @computed_field property cors_origins
  that accepts JSON arrays, comma-separated values, or a single URL
- Update main.py to use settings.cors_origins (the parsed list)
- Update .env.example to document both accepted formats

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix code review issues: security, data integrity, and reliability

- Fix SQLite DB path to align with K8s volume mount (/app/data)
- Reduce backend replicas to 1 (SQLite single-writer limitation)
- Fix CORS: disable credentials with wildcard origins
- Add try/except with db.rollback() on all write operations
- Use UTC-aware datetimes for all date calculations
- Add regex validation on currency symbol input
- Handle delete errors in History page with user feedback
- Disable ACR admin user (managed identity handles auth)
- Create /app/data dir in Dockerfile
